goal: reconcile D1 design and preserve wave recovery map

summary.title: 整合 D1 设计并补充 wave 恢复入口
summary.what: Reconciled the D1 architecture design packet from the execution thread into the canonical workflow control plane, updated registry/state to mark D1 verified and Wave 3 ready, and added wave-map.md as the compact recovery point for future context compaction.
summary.why: The orchestrator must not rely on chat context or a worker worktree for the remaining waves. A canonical design and wave map keep the multi-source objective, locked decisions, execution order, and stop lines durable.
summary.user_goal: reconcile D1 design and preserve wave recovery map

decisions.chose: Copied the D1 design and handoff into the canonical docs/workflows/2026-06-05-session-sources control plane.
decisions.rationale: Worker outputs are not authoritative until the orchestrator reconciles them back into the control plane.
rejected: Leaving D1 only in the worker worktree
Relying on the chat summary as the design source

decisions.chose: Added wave-map.md as the shortest restart surface for the remaining waves.
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly called out the risk of losing the goal after context grows.
rejected: Adding more prose only to workflow-state.md

decisions.chose: Marked I1 ready first, with I2/I3 gated by I1 and storage/selector contracts.
decisions.rationale: The adapter boundary should be established before source-aware persistence and CLI behavior are wired.

rejected.reason: That would make the implementation wave depend on non-canonical worker state.

review_notes: Docs/control-plane checkpoint only; no source code, schema, CLI, release skill, npm package, global install, push, or PR changes.
Validation: git diff --check passed before commit.
Mainline prepare reports the full branch diff against origin/main; the actual latest commit is docs(workflow): 整合多源设计恢复点.

architectural_claims: Canonical workflow design now states source_id as selector, coverage, DB, query, and read dimension.
wave-map.md records the locked decisions and next execution order for context recovery.
security_implications: No runtime security behavior changed; Claude Code remains non-public and not indexed.
migration_notes: No migration implemented in this checkpoint; migration strategy is described in design.md for later I2 work.
